caveats: a: OEWS evidence covers NAICS 3221 rather than 322110 and does not measure task exposure directly.
a: The frozen labor ratio uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and a cross-industry harmonization divisor.
rho: No representative five-year adoption study specific to US pulp mills was found.
rho: Autonomous process control is treated conservatively because safety and emissions accountability remain with the operator.
e: The dataset estimates only five LMM firms using a sector margin bridge; pulp-mill capital intensity can make that bridge materially misleading.
e: Recurring market-pulp sales are not automatically recurring outsourced services.
s5: General small-business evidence is especially weak for a capital-intensive process industry.
s5: The five-firm dataset estimate creates high discrete-event uncertainty.
q: No public contract sample for LMM independent mills was found.
q: Energy, chemicals, fiber, freight, and currency can swamp an AI-related cost change in observed pricing.
d5: Industrial production is a broad output index, not constant-quality demand for the qualifying service basket.
d5: Grade conversions can make aggregate tonnage a poor proxy for economic demand.
o: The estimate concerns accountable operator need, not preservation of current staffing.
o: Integrated buyers and consolidation can replace the current firm type without eliminating physical mill operation.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Qualifying mills earn contract, tolling, or repeated per-ton supply revenue from industrial customers; pricing may be fixed, indexed, cost-plus, or market-linked.
lens.deviation_from_default: Pulp is a commodity intermediate rather than a conventional service, and many mills are captive or much larger than the target band. The lens is narrowed to independent mills with recurring outsourced-production relationships.
lens.excluded_activities: Integrated captive pulp operations, spot-only commodity sellers, one-off product transactions, non-operating assets, shells, financing vehicles, and firms outside the EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market stand-alone pulp producers only where paper, packaging, tissue, specialty-fiber, or other industrial customers repeatedly outsource fiber production under ongoing supply or toll-processing relationships.

a.bridge: BLS reports that other production occupations are 42.60% of employment in the broader pulp, paper, and paperboard mill industry, while EPA describes kraft pulping as chemical treatment under high pressure with regulated combustion units. I translate that operator-, maintenance-, and process-heavy mix into low direct generative-AI exposure, concentrated in planning, procurement, lab and compliance documentation, predictive analysis, and administration. The broader 3221 mix may differ from stand-alone pulp mills, while control-room work may be more AI-augmentable than its production label suggests.
caveats: OEWS evidence covers NAICS 3221 rather than 322110 and does not measure task exposure directly.
The frozen labor ratio uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and a cross-industry harmonization divisor.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted six-digit staffing table and task-time study at stand-alone chemical, mechanical, recycled, and specialty pulp mills, including control-room and maintenance work.
a.rationale: Continuous chemical processing, material handling, utilities, maintenance, safety, lab testing, and environmental controls dominate. AI can reduce analytical and administrative labor and improve decision support, but cannot replace most accountable plant operation.

d5.bridge: Federal Reserve G.17 publishes a separate pulp-mill industrial-production series and EPA identifies market pulp, integrated pulp and paper, recycled fiber, and purchased-pulp operations across the sector. I use the mature, volatile physical-output series as the anchor and apply a broad five-year range reflecting decline in graphic-paper demand, offsetting packaging/tissue/specialty uses, imports, exports, recycling, and closures. Aggregate production does not isolate qualifying LMM outsourced suppliers and may understate niches with favorable grade mix.
caveats: Industrial production is a broad output index, not constant-quality demand for the qualifying service basket.
Grade conversions can make aggregate tonnage a poor proxy for economic demand.
d5.change_evidence: Fastmarkets or RISI five-year tonnage forecasts by pulp grade and end use, reconciled with G.17 output, trade flows, announced closures, conversions, and the qualifying customer mix.
d5.rationale: Digitization continues to pressure printing and writing end uses, while packaging, tissue, absorbent, specialty cellulose, and fiber substitution support others. Domestic demand is also exposed to global pulp capacity, freight, fiber costs, and irreversible mill closures.

caveats: The dataset estimates only five LMM firms using a sector margin bridge; pulp-mill capital intensity can make that bridge materially misleading.
Recurring market-pulp sales are not automatically recurring outsourced services.
e.change_evidence: Facility-by-facility ownership, capacity, customer-contract, and revenue-model diligence for the dataset-estimated five LMM firms, explicitly identifying captive versus market and tolling output.
e.rationale: Most pulp output is either an integrated captive input or a commodity product sale, neither of which fits the recurring outsourced-service lens cleanly. A small subset of independent specialty, recycled-fiber, tolling, or contract-supply mills may qualify.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 322110 ($444,101,381) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 322110 ($8,617,887,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1465 (margins.json[32]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Paper/Forest Products EBITDA/Sales 14.65% (mid-range for wood/paper/plastics)); revenue/employee $911,656 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 322110); class <5: 5 firms, avg revenue/firm $459,800 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $67,361 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 5 firms, avg revenue/firm $26,084,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,821,306 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 5 firms, avg revenue/firm $84,747,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,415,436 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 8 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,007,573,125 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $147,609,463 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 5

o.bridge: EPA describes physical chemical conversion of wood chips into pulp, pressurized cooking, recovery furnaces, lime kilns, emissions limits, and facility reporting. I map those required processes and accountabilities to a near-total need for a plant operator for surviving quantity. The proxy could overstate persistence of independent operators if customers integrate supply or plants consolidate.
caveats: The estimate concerns accountable operator need, not preservation of current staffing.
Integrated buyers and consolidation can replace the current firm type without eliminating physical mill operation.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of customer vertical integration, contract manufacturing replacement, fully remote autonomous-mill operation, or regulatory changes shifting operating responsibility.
o.rationale: Software cannot convert fiber into marketable pulp or hold permits and product responsibility. AI may reduce staffing and centralize expertise, but nearly all surviving tons still require a mill organization operating physical and environmental systems.

caveats: No public contract sample for LMM independent mills was found.
Energy, chemicals, fiber, freight, and currency can swamp an AI-related cost change in observed pricing.
q.change_evidence: Customer-contract samples identifying market-index, cost-plus, fixed-price, minimum-volume, energy-surcharge, and annual-reset provisions, linked to realized mill margins.
q.rationale: Market-linked per-ton pricing and sophisticated industrial buyers transmit productivity gains through index resets, negotiations, and global competition. Specialty grades, tolling contracts, reliability, fiber access, and long qualification cycles can protect some benefit; explicit cost-plus terms can pass it through quickly.

caveats: No representative five-year adoption study specific to US pulp mills was found.
Autonomous process control is treated conservatively because safety and emissions accountability remain with the operator.
rho.change_evidence: Verified plant trials showing hours avoided and sustained performance from AI maintenance, process advisory, energy optimization, laboratory, and compliance systems.
rho.rationale: Maintenance planning, anomaly triage, energy optimization, purchasing, lab interpretation, environmental reporting, and work-order knowledge are plausible five-year uses. Legacy control systems, scarce labeled failure data, cyber risk, 24/7 reliability, safety, environmental permits, and high error costs slow implementation.

s5.bridge: Gallup measures five-year transfer plans among US employer businesses and BizBuySell records small-manufacturing transactions. I use those broad populations as a prior, then discount for the tiny estimated firm universe, specialized assets, environmental liabilities, strategic ownership, capital requirements, and mill-closure risk. Small marketplace deals likely overstate liquidity for regulated pulp assets.
caveats: General small-business evidence is especially weak for a capital-intensive process industry.
The five-firm dataset estimate creates high discrete-event uncertainty.
s5.change_evidence: A ten-year facility-level control, closure, and restart database from Fastmarkets, PitchBook, RISI, regulatory permit records, and transaction filings, paired with owner interviews.
s5.rationale: Qualifying control transfers can occur through strategic divestitures or owner succession, but a pulp mill's site, permits, fiber basket, energy system, environmental history, and customer contracts sharply constrain buyers. Closure or asset sale may fail the transferable-operation test.

principal_driver: High-value maintenance, process, energy, and compliance decisions provide a focused AI-assistance opportunity.
principal_weakness: Very few LMM pulp mills are independent recurring outsourced-service providers, and their economics are dominated by assets and commodities rather than labor.
